covered_sentences: When Hareton had read the passage through with no mistakes, Catherine kissed him and said, Let's go for our walk now.
I did not want to interrupt them, so I hid behind the door until they were gone.
When they were out of the gate, I looked into the sitting room again and saw my old friend Nelly Dean sitting by the fire, sewing.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right split, 1:1. Left panel: mid-shot of the sitting room from just inside the doorway, camera angled toward the central hearth area; a middle-aged woman sits on a wooden chair close to the fireplace, back slightly toward the viewer at a three-quarter angle, needle and thread in hand, firelight casting warm light on her side profile. Right panel: camera positioned outside the doorway at low 20° inward angle, showing the narrator's hands gripping the doorframe edge as foreground — the narrator peeks around the door edge, body hidden; through the gap the two young figures (not identifiable in close-up, rendered as receding shapes) are shown moving toward the gate in the background.

Left panel: Nelly Dean sits on a hard wooden chair beside the fireplace, right hand holding a needle, left hand steadying cloth on her lap; she looks downward at her sewing, relaxed posture, fire glow visible to her right. Right panel: narrator's hands grip the door edge at foreground right; narrator's eye visible around the edge; in the background two indistinct figures — one taller broad-shouldered, one petite curly-haired — walk side by side toward the outer gate, backs to viewer.

Left panel: Wuthering Heights sitting room, late afternoon transitioning to early evening, firelight dominant, ambient daylight through the deep-set window fading; Nelly sits adjacent to the fireplace, the iron grate and firelight glow to her immediate right; the stone floor and oak cabinet are visible in the background. Right panel: just outside the front doorway, soft outdoor late-afternoon light; stone door frame as foreground; courtyard path and gate visible beyond the two receding figures.

Left panel: warm amber firelight on Nelly and the hearth area, cool stone walls in shadow. Right panel: soft grey-blue outdoor ambient, silhouetted figures against the lighter courtyard beyond.

Left panel: Nelly Dean — deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face, dark domestic dress with white apron, ribbon at collar; she sits, and her head height is approximately at the level of the top of the fireplace mantle. Right panel: only Nelly's absence is implied by the empty chair seen distantly; the narrator is represented only by hands gripping the door edge and one eye visible; the two departing young figures in the background are not identifiable as character_refs — rendered as silhouettes, not requiring reference-accurate depiction.

Scene fingerprint: large stone fireplace with iron grate + oak cabinet with tin plates at left corner + stone-flagged white floor, positions unchanged. Nelly Dean: hair must stay dark brown and pinned up, white apron must not be removed, dark dress must not be replaced. No floating text, no narration captions, no title text, no label text. Nelly seated — her head height approximately at mantle level. Departing figures in background are non-identifiable silhouettes, not written into character_refs.

covered_sentences: She saw me and leapt to her feet.
Mr. Lockwood, why didn't you tell us you were coming?
Thrushcross Grange will not be ready for you.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right dialogue split, 2:1 ratio (Nelly dominant, as her surprise reaction and speech carry this beat). Left panel: camera positioned at mid-distance inside the sitting room, angled from slightly behind the narrator's left shoulder toward Nelly Dean who has just leapt to her feet; Nelly faces the viewer at roughly three-quarter angle, wide-eyed and upright, the wooden chair just pushed back behind her. Right panel: close-up over-Nelly's-shoulder angle looking at the narrator's face — mild surprise at her reaction, politely composed.

Left panel: Nelly Dean stands abruptly, both hands slightly raised at waist height as if caught mid-motion of standing; speech bubble above her head contains a simplified manor-house outline sketch + question mark icon. Right panel: narrator faces Nelly, expression calm but slightly startled; no speech bubble in this panel.

Left panel: Wuthering Heights sitting room, daytime interior light from the deep window, fireplace behind Nelly to the left; wooden chair is displaced slightly behind her as she has stood up. Right panel: same room, reverse over-shoulder; Nelly's dark-coated shoulder occupies the left foreground, background reveals a narrow slice of the stone wall and window.

Left panel: soft interior light, mild warmth from the fireplace side, neutral daytime ambient. Right panel: same interior ambient, Nelly's silhouette as dark foreground framing the narrator's lit face.

Left panel: Nelly Dean — deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face showing surprise, dark domestic dress with white apron; she stands, head height approximately at the fireplace mantle level, chair seat visible behind her at about her mid-thigh height. Right panel: narrator (Lockwood) — light/brown short hair, dark coat and collar, face fills approximately 65% of the panel height; Nelly's shoulder occupies lower-left foreground at roughly one-quarter of panel width.

covered_sentences: I have been to Thrushcross Grange, and the housekeeper is preparing a room for me now.
But tell me, why aren't you at Thrushcross Grange, Mrs. Dean?

covered_sentences: Oh, I've been here for over three months now.
I came just before Mr. Heathcliff died, she replied.
